text: The Femring is great because you insert it and don't have to worry about remembering to take a pill or pills every day. This allows you freedom for the next three months, (90) days until your reorder a new ring. Also my insurance pays a good portion of the cost of the ring, whereas it did not cover the cost of bio-identical horomone treatments. The bad thing about this certain dosage of the Femring was on the third day it seemed as though I was beginning to have vaginal irritation. The fourth day was obvious vaginal irritation with a blistery rash. Upon feeling and seeing this rash I removed the Femring and called my Dr. A lower dose Femring was prescribed, 0.05mg/day, which does a good job of returning my horomones levels to close to normal.
